title: carduus × schulzeanus subsp. laxus
intro: Carduus × schulzeanus subsp. laxus, also known as Schulze's thistle, is a biennial or perennial plant in the sunflower family that is often found in dry, rocky areas. Traditionally, Schulze's thistle has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as exhibiting potential antiviral and antibacterial properties. In herbal preparations, Schulze's thistle is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support immune function and overall well-being.
